notes: Very comprehensive baseline. Adds: commit signing (GPG/SSH), pip-audit + safety, semgrep with PCI-DSS rules, team-based CODEOWNERS, SIEM streaming, access control. Better CODEOWNERS.
Tier system assertion (named tier recommendation) passes 100% with_skill, 0% without_skill — the most discriminating assertion across all evals
Effort estimates assertion passes 100% with_skill, 0% without_skill — perfectly discriminating, no skill ever adds effort estimates
Pre-commit config (black+ruff) passes ~100% in both configurations — non-discriminating, both give good pre-commit advice
WHY explanations pass ~87% in both configurations — non-discriminating, both explain reasoning

Branch protection priority: eval-1 WITHOUT skill correctly puts branch protection first (for an existing team with incidents), while WITH skill puts pre-commit first per its fixed 'order of operations' principle — skill's ordering is wrong for this context
Baseline fintech (eval-2) adds commit signing, pip-audit, semgrep with PCI-DSS rulesets, team-based CODEOWNERS — areas where skill could improve for regulated environments
Skill uses ~36% more tokens (+4729 mean) and takes ~14.7s longer — cost of structured output vs direct response
